en.extract:
The São Tomé shorttail (Motacilla bocagii), also known as Bocage's longbill, is a species of passerine bird in the family Motacillidae. It has been classified as the sole member of the genus Amaurocichla, but a 2015 phylogenetic study placed it among the wagtails in the genus Motacilla. It is endemic to the central and southern parts of the island of São Tomé. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests. This species has a small population and is threatened by habitat loss.


References

Eccles, S. D. (1988). "The birds of São Tomé — record of a visit, April 1987 with notes on the rediscovery of Bocage's Longbill" (PDF). Malimbus. 10 (2): 207–217.
Johansson, U. S.; Fjeldså, J.; Bowie, R. C. K. (2008). "Phylogenetic relationships within Passerida (Aves: Passeriformes): A review and a new molecular phylogeny based on three nuclear intron markers". Molecular Phylogenetics and Evolution. 48 (3): 858–876. doi:10.1016/j.ympev.2008.05.029. PMID 18619860.
Kingdon, Jonathan (1989). Island Africa: The Evolution of Africa's Rare Plants and Animals. Princeton, New Jersey: Princeton University Press. pp. 37–38. ISBN 0-691-08560-9.

zh.extract:
包氏鹡鸰（学名：Motacilla bocagii）为雀形目下的一个种。包氏鹡鸰为圣多美和普林西比的特有种，其自然栖息地位于亚热带或热带潮湿的低地森林中。目前它们正在受到栖息地丧失的威胁。


参考文献

Eccles, S. D. The birds of São Tomé — record of a visit, April 1987 with notes on the rediscovery of Bocage’s Longbill (PDF). Malimbus. 1988, 10 (2): 207–217  [2014-08-25]. （原始内容存档 (PDF)于2018-09-28）. 
Johansson, U. S.; Fjeldså, J.; Bowie, R. C. K. Phylogenetic relationships within Passerida (Aves: Passeriformes): A review and a new molecular phylogeny based on three nuclear intron markers. Molecular Phylogenetics and Evolution. 2008, 48 (3): 858–876. PMID 18619860. doi:10.1016/j.ympev.2008.05.029.  
Kingdon, Jonathan. Island Africa: The Evolution of Africa's Rare Plants and Animals. Princeton, New Jersey: Princeton University Press. 1989: 37–38. ISBN 0-691-08560-9.
